taxonomy: Pentaria orbiculata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, which is commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it within the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. Pentaria orbiculata is further classified as a member of the tribe Gnaphalieae, a group of plants that are commonly found in dry, rocky areas. The genus Pentaria is part of the larger family Asteraceae and is native to Australia. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the same family and tribe.
